items.title: Police charge 13yo girl after alleged crime rampage in Melbourne
items.summary: Victoria Police say the teenager was driving a stolen car when she swerved towards a group of people from the Jewish community on Saturday.

items.title: WA's greyhound racing regulator defends track record at inquiry into sport's future
items.summary: The body governing greyhound racing in WA says it is determined to maintain the welfare of both the dogs and the industry at a parliamentary inquiry, which was sparked by a petition that garnered 26,000 signatures.

items.title: Family of Zomi Frankcom still waiting for answers two years after death at hands of IDF
items.summary: Two years ago Australian aid worker Zomi Frankcom was killed by an IDF strike in Gaza. Her family is still waiting for answers and accountability.

items.title: Thousands of Chinese social media users change profile photos to Kris Jenner
items.summary: Thousands of Chinese social media users have started using images of US celebrity Kris Jenner as their profile photos in hopes it will bring them success and good fortune.

items.title: WA man Samuel Jingle sentenced to life in prison after killing woman in state's north
items.summary: Samuel Jade Lincoln Jingle will have to serve a minimum of 19 years in prison after killing his partner in Fitzroy Crossing.

items.title: Australian water polo star switches allegiances to Croatia ahead of World Cup campaign
items.summary: Luke Pavillard, who starred for the Aussie Sharks Water Polo team at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games, says he will be switching his national allegiance to Croatia.

items.title: Zac Lomax available to make Super Rugby Pacific debut if selected for Western Force
items.summary: Zac Lomax could get his first taste of rugby union at a venue where the Western Force recruit is no stranger to receiving a hostile welcome.

items.title: ABC proposes new pay offer to staff after 24-hour strike
items.summary: The updated ABC offer includes new provisions to ensure staff progress through pay bandings.

items.title: Why these women have never had a partner
items.summary: Three women share their experiences of never having a partner — not by choice — and what that's like when you wish you did.

items.title: 'Welcome to Country' pioneer farewelled on her beloved Bundjalung Country
items.summary: Rhoda Roberts AO is being remembered for her ability to laugh and embrace life, as well as her contribution to the Australian arts and Indigenous cultural movements.

items.title: Disability agency adds nearly 2,000 new employees while trying to rein in costs
items.summary: The agency that runs the National Disability Insurance Scheme defends hiring nearly 2,000 new employees last year despite major changes being rolled out to rein in the program's cost.

items.title: Salary dramas, blown draft picks and scandals — How Essendon and Carlton found themselves in AFL purgatory
items.summary: Essendon and Carlton were once mainstays when it came to the business end of the AFL season. Over the last quarter century, both clubs have fallen on hard times. Here's why.

items.title: Young farmers embracing latest agricultural technology with drones and apps
items.summary: A shift towards modern technology is making a career in agriculture more appealing to a new generation of farmers.

items.title: Is this image of a child climbing on a missile real? ABC NEWS Verify explains
items.summary: Huge amounts of false images and video circulating about the conflict in Iran are often making it difficult to distinguish genuine news from manipulated fakery, but this one is real.

items.title: Tim Rogers on making a living, fatherhood and a You Am I classic
items.summary: The frontman of ARIA Hall Of Famers You Am I on work, family, and why Hourly, Daily is still special to him.

items.title: NASA begins countdown for humanity's first launch to the moon in 53 years
items.summary: Four astronauts on the Artemis II rocket are set to blast off early Thursday morning.

items.title: How Porepunkah learned the search for Dezi Freeman was over
items.summary: Seven months after a gunman killed two police officers on a nearby property, the tiny town woke to news Dezi Freeman was dead.

items.title: Fair Work Commission abolishes junior pay rates for young adult employees
items.summary: The Fair Work Commission has issued its decision to abolish junior pay rates for young adult employees in the retail, fast food, and pharmacy sectors while maintaining them for minors.

items.title: Jackie 'O' Henderson files legal action against ARN Media
items.summary: Former Sydney radio broadcaster Jackie "O" Henderson has filed legal action against employer ARN Media, following the collapse of the hit Kyle and Jackie O Show.

items.title: Live: Israel intends to apply Gaza 'model' in Lebanon
items.summary: Israel is planning to occupy Lebanon's south and repeat what it did in Gaza, according to Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz. Follow live.

items.title: The oil shock has completely reshaped Australia's petrol price cycle
items.summary: Petrol stations in our biggest cities hiked prices ahead of global oil prices, but experts say their profit margins are now much slimmer.

items.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Matthew Doran
items.summary: Donald Trump renews threats against Iran's energy infrastructure while his secretary of war faces an apparent rebuke from the pope.

items.title: Israel passes death penalty for Palestinians convicted of lethal attacks
items.summary: The law, long-sought by Israel's far-right politicians, makes death by hanging the default punishment for West Bank Palestinians convicted of killing Israelis.

items.title: The Matildas hype is real. So why are the A-League Women crowds still so small?
items.summary: Despite the recent hype of the Matildas, support for the A-League Women remains relatively low.

items.title: Italy's World Cup fate to be decided, Socceroos to learn final group opponent
items.summary: The last of the 48 teams for the FIFA World Cup will be decided tomorrow, with Italy hoping to end a 12-year exile, while Türkiye and Kosovo look to join the Socceroos' group.

items.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iranian oil and energy sites if it refuses deal to end war
items.summary: As thousands more US troops arrive in the Middle East, Donald Trump threatens to wipe out Iran's energy plants and oil wells if it does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: From tax cuts to price gouging crackdowns, how G7 nations are combating the fuel crisis
items.summary: Australia is not alone in weathering rising fuel costs and issues with domestic supply and distribution amid the war in the Middle East. G7 nations are also working out ways to ease the crisis.

items.title: Dezi Freeman's death gives 'closure' to slain Victorian police officer Neal Thompson's friend
items.summary: The best friend of slain Porepunkah detective Neal Thompson says the police shooting of fugitive gunman Dezi Freeman gives "closure", though it will not bring back his mate.

items.title: Israel Defense Forces admit to posting fake image of Lebanese journalist killed in strike
items.summary: The IDF has admitted the image of a killed journalist in military uniform it posted on its official X account was faked.

items.title: Government halves fuel excise for three months to ease cost of petrol, diesel
items.summary: The government has halved the fuel excise on petrol and diesel for three months, reducing the cost of fuel by 26.3 cents per litre.

items.title: Antarctic penguin appears on remote WA beach, 4,000km from home
items.summary: The king penguin, known for being the second-largest penguin species and living on ice-free parts of the sub-Antarctic, was rescued near Esperance on WA's south coast.

items.title: Dezi Freeman killed by police in Victoria's north-east after seven-month hunt
items.summary: Freeman had been wanted since last August over the fatal shooting of two police officers at Porepunkah.

items.title: Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub closing due to lack of funding, leaving First Nations patients in Canberra in the lurch
items.summary: An absence of funding from the ACT and federal governments is forcing the Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub to close this week, affecting about 400 First Nations patients.

items.title: How to approach your employer about working from home and flexible work arrangements
items.summary: With rising fuel prices adding additional financial stress to household living costs, what are your rights when it comes to working from home — and what are the best ways to approach your employer about flexible work arrangements?

items.title: From Australian Idol to The Piano, Guy Sebastian has tried most types of reality TV
items.summary: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

items.title: End of an era for Tharwa General Store after a century being owned by one family
items.summary: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

items.title: Mobile coverage is an election issue in regional areas. What's been promised?
items.summary: For many regional and remote Australians, reliable mobile coverage can be life-saving. They are desperate for both sides of politics to take meaningful action towards eliminating black spots.

items.title: Housing advocates seek federal funding election promise for co-op model
items.summary: Advocates admit co-operative housing models are not for everyone but say for the right residents, they offer more security and affordable pricing than traditional rentals.

items.title: Australia's growing cohort of tenants fear they will rent forever
items.summary: Home ownership rates in Australia have been falling for decades, and with rental vacancies at record lows and prices at historic highs, many fear their dream of owning a home may be out of reach. Some experts say rental caps could help.

items.title: Australians tell ABC's Your Say they are struggling more than ever with housing costs
items.summary: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they are struggling more than ever with housing costs.

items.title: Push for seniors' dental scheme grows ahead of federal election
items.summary: Imagine living in aged care and not having your teeth or dentures properly cleaned and checked each day. That's a risk faced by elderly Australians, prompting calls for the next federal government to boost oral health training and services.

items.title: Voters tell ABC's Your Say they want politicians with a vision, not bickering
items.summary: Voters tell ABC's Your Say campaign they want courage and vision from politicians, not bickering.

items.title: Have Your Say: What was the issue, policy or event that decided your vote?
items.summary: After a five-week campaign, the results are in with Labor returned to government. Is there a policy or issue that swayed your vote? Have Your Say.

items.title: The US election is about style more than substance. It's why Kamala Harris needs to nail her DNC speech in about eight seconds
items.summary: When the Democrat presidential candidate Kamala Harris delivers her speech to the Democratic National Convention today, style will be almost everything.
